message.content:
{
  "homework": {
    "sessionInformation": {
      "sessionNumber": "1",
      "sessionTitle": "Number System",
      "subject": "Mathematics",
      "grade": "Class IX",
      "difficultyLevel": "Balanced",
      "learningPace": "Balanced",
      "estimatedHomeworkDuration": "45 minutes"
    },
    "homework": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "type": "Numerical Problem",
        "title": "Decimal Representation of Irrational Numbers",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Identify irrational numbers", "Represent irrational numbers on the number line"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Irrational Numbers", "Real Number System"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 2,
        "estimatedTime": "8 minutes",
        "instructions": "Convert the following irrational numbers into their decimal forms up to three places after the decimal point. Then, identify which of these are non-terminating and non-recurring.",
        "question": "a) √2\nb) √3\nc) π (take 3.141)",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write your answers in the space provided below each question.",
        "visualRequirement": "Draw a number line and mark the approximate positions of √2 and √3.",
        "expectedResponse": "a) 1.414, b) 1.732, c) 3.141. All are non-terminating and non-recurring."
      },
      {
        "id": 2,
        "type": "Assertion Reason",
        "title": "Rationality of Square Roots",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Distinguish between rational and irrational numbers"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Rational Numbers", "Irrational Numbers"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 2,
        "estimatedTime": "6 minutes",
        "instructions": "Read the Assertion (A) and Reason (R) carefully. Choose the correct option from the given choices.",
        "question": "Assertion (A): The square root of any prime number is irrational.\nReason (R): Prime numbers have exactly two factors, 1 and themselves.",
        "options": [
          "Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A",
          "Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A",
          "A is true but R is false",
          "A is false but R is true"
        ],
        "answerSpace": "Select the correct option.",
        "visualRequirement": "",
        "expectedResponse": "Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A."
      },
      {
        "id": 3,
        "type": "Short Answer",
        "title": "Real World Application of Irrational Numbers",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Apply number system concepts to real-world scenarios"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Real Number System", "Applications"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 3,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Explain why the diagonal of a square with side length 1 meter cannot be expressed as a fraction. Use the Pythagorean theorem in your explanation.",
        "question": "Calculate the length of the diagonal and explain its nature (rational or irrational).",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write a short paragraph explaining your reasoning.",
        "visualRequirement": "Draw a square with side 1m and label the diagonal.",
        "expectedResponse": "Diagonal = √(1² + 1²) = √2. Since √2 cannot be written as p/q where q≠0, it is irrational."
      },
      {
        "id": 4,
        "type": "Numerical Problem",
        "title": "Operations with Irrational Numbers",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Perform operations on real numbers"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Real Number System", "Operations"],
        "difficulty": "Hard",
        "marks": 3,
        "estimatedTime": "12 minutes",
        "instructions": "Simplify the following expressions and state whether the result is rational or irrational.",
        "question": "a) √2 + √3\nb) √8 × √2\nc) (√5 - 2)(√5 + 2)",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Show your steps and state the nature of the result.",
        "visualRequirement": "",
        "expectedResponse": "a) Irrational, b) Rational (4), c) Rational (1)."
      },
      {
        "id": 5,
        "type": "Observation to Conclusion",
        "title": "Pattern Recognition in Number Systems",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Analyze patterns in number systems"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Number System Patterns"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 2,
        "estimatedTime": "9 minutes",
        "instructions": "Observe the following sequence of numbers. Identify if they are rational or irrational and justify your answer.",
        "question": "Sequence: 0.1010010001..., √4, 22/7, π, 0.333...",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Create a table with columns: Number, Type (Rational/Irrational), Reason.",
        "visualRequirement": "Draw the table as described.",
        "expectedResponse": "Table showing classification of each number with reasons."
      }
    ],
    "summary": {
      "totalQuestions": 5,
      "totalMarks": 12,
      "estimatedCompletionTime": "45 minutes",
      "learningOutcomesCovered": [
        "Identify irrational numbers",
        "Represent irrational numbers on the number line",
        "Distinguish between rational and irrational numbers",
        "Apply number system concepts to real-world scenarios",
        "Perform operations on real numbers",
        "Analyze patterns in number systems"
      ],
      "topicsCovered": [
        "Irrational Numbers",
        "Real Number System",
        "Rational Numbers",
        "Applications",
        "Operations",
        "Number System Patterns"
      ],
      "subtopicsCovered": [
        "Decimal Representation",
        "Square Roots of Primes",
        "Pythagorean Theorem Application",
        "Algebraic Operations",
        "Pattern Analysis"
      ],
      "taskDistribution": {
        "Numerical Problem": 2,
        "Assertion Reason": 1,
        "Short Answer": 1,
        "Observation to Conclusion": 1
      },
      "homeExperimentIncluded": false,
      "parentEngagementIncluded": false
    }
  }
}
